params.prompt: Vertical portrait brand social tile for a barbershop, near 4:5 aspect, cover/title card for a carousel. Background: a 2x3 photographic mosaic/collage grid built from the five attached reference photos (reference images 1 through 5), each cell showing a tight crop of one of those exact scenes -- a fade haircut with clippers, a beard being shaped with a razor and comb, a facial cleansing foam application, a manicure moment, and a man resting in a massage chair with an eye mask -- keep each cell's own high-contrast black and white duotone grading with warm golden-yellow rim light exactly as it already looks in those reference photos, thin ink-black (#101010) gutter lines separating the grid cells, no straight color photography. Add a dark ink (#101010) gradient scrim across the lower half of the whole tile for text legibility. Top-left: a small solid Nordic-yellow (#FFD200) pill-shaped tag containing bold condensed uppercase sans-serif text 'CUIDADO PERSONAL' in dark ink letters, tight letter tracking. Center-lower area, on top of the scrim: a large headline reading '5 HABITOS DEL HOMBRE MODERNO', set in the hand-cut marker lettering style shown in the seventh reference image (a font character map) -- reproduce that exact letterform style, all uppercase, tight line height, wrapping across two or three lines as needed -- where the word MODERNO is solid Nordic yellow (#FFD200) and the rest of the words are solid bone-white (#F7F4EC), no other colors on the headline. Directly below the headline, one short line of clean condensed grotesk sans-serif body text in bone-white at reduced opacity, reading: 'Del corte a la calma: las rutinas que marcan la diferencia. Desliza para verlas.' Bottom-right corner: place the exact logo lockup shown in the sixth reference image (a white Viking helmet mark beside the words NORDIC / Barber Shop, already on a black background) completely unchanged and at small watermark scale -- do not invent, redraw, alter proportions, recolor, or add glow/shadow to this logo; blend its black background seamlessly into the tile's own ink-black canvas so no visible box or seam appears around it. Keep the overall canvas ink-black (#101010) dominant, yellow only as a small accent never exceeding about 10-15% of the frame.
